title: ranunculus flammula var. major
intro: Ranunculus flammula var. major, also known as greater water crowfoot, is a perennial plant species in the buttercup family that is native to wetlands and waterways in Europe and Western Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus flammula var. major include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to reap its health benefits.
